summary: CLI fetches and eval()s unpinned JavaScript from unpkg.com at every invocation
details: On every CLI invocation (hive/solve/task and related entry points), the package fetches https://unpkg.com/use-m/use.js — an unpinned URL with no version specifier and no integrity check — and passes the response body directly to eval() to obtain a `use` loader. The pattern is repeated across ~30 files (src/lib.mjs:35, src/hive.mjs:48-54, src/claude.lib.mjs:5, src/solve.bootstrap.lib.mjs:23, etc.). Because the URL is unpinned, unpkg serves whatever is currently published as the latest `use-m`; any future malicious publish of use-m, registry tampering, unpkg cache poisoning, or MITM results in arbitrary JavaScript executing in the developer's shell context, with access to the developer's GitHub, AWS, npm, and SSH credentials. The eval is not gated by an install lifecycle hook, but fires unconditionally on tool startup, so any developer who installs this package and runs its documented CLI is exposed. Separately, an opt-in `--auto-cleanup` flag shells out `sudo rm -rf /tmp/* /var/tmp/*`; user-gated and not the basis for this verdict, but worth noting as an aggressive-destructive option.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
